# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a Python data processing application. Start from a full Ubuntu base image and install Python, pip, curl, wget, git, vim, and other useful development tools. This gives us flexibility for debugging and running various scripts in production.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for container security and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any insecure patterns relevant to this vulnerability class.
4. Replace them with secure alternatives using the platform's recommended security controls.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: container_security

# Initial implementation with security review completed

FROM ubuntu:22.04

# Set non-interactive frontend to avoid prompts during build
ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive \
    PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 \
    PIP_NO_CACHE_DIR=1 \
    PIP_DISABLE_PIP_VERSION_CHECK=1

# Create non-root user first
RUN groupadd -r appuser -g 1000 && \
    useradd -r -u 1000 -g appuser -m -s /bin/bash appuser

# Update system and install packages in a single layer, then clean up
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    python3=3.10.6-1~22.04 \
    python3-pip=22.0.2+dfsg-1ubuntu0.4 \
    python3-venv=3.10.6-1~22.04 \
    curl=7.81.0-1ubuntu1.16 \
    wget=1.21.2-2ubuntu1 \
    git=1:2.34.1-1ubuntu1.11 \
    vim-tiny=2:8.2.3995-1ubuntu2.17 \
    ca-certificates=20230311ubuntu0.22.04.1 && \
    apt-get clean && \
    rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/* /tmp/* /var/tmp/* && \
    ln -s /usr/bin/python3 /usr/bin/python

# Set working directory with proper permissions
WORKDIR /app
RUN chown appuser:appuser /app

# Switch to non-root user
USER appuser

# Create virtual environment for Python packages
RUN python -m venv /home/appuser/venv
ENV PATH="/home/appuser/venv/bin:$PATH"

# Upgrade pip in virtual environment
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir --upgrade pip==24.0

# Set secure umask
RUN echo "umask 027" >> /home/appuser/.bashrc

# Health check
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=3s --start-period=5s --retries=3 \
    CMD python -c "print('healthy')" || exit 1

CMD ["/bin/bash"]
